issues.title: Can't delete and separate words by Scribble with Apple Pencil written on TextFormField when setting maxLines 1
issues.body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Run the app with the below sample code.
2. Write some words with Apple Pencil on TextFormField with the label `TextFormField with maxLines: 1`.
3. Scratch words or draw the vertical line between characters.

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
When scratching words, then it is removed from TextFormField, and when drawing the vertical line between characters, then the space is inserted, just like the demo which can be tried from the Apple Pencil setting on iPad.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/125345277/222701827-b3a9df28-69ff-4b83-8da4-1f7eddd20f55.MP4



<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

<!--
      Please create a minimal reproducible sample that shows the problem
      and attach it below between the lines with the backticks.

      To create it you can use `flutter create bug` command and update the `main.dart` file.

      Alternatively, you can use https://dartpad.dev/
      which is capable of creating and running small Flutter apps.

      Without this we will unlikely be able to progress on the issue, and because of that
      we regretfully will have to close it.
-->

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
      ),
      home: const MainPage(),
    );
  }
}

class MainPage extends StatefulWidget {
  const MainPage({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  _MainPageState createState() => _MainPageState();
}

class _MainPageState extends State<MainPage> {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      body: ListView(
        padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(vertical: 64, horizontal: 16),
        children: [
          TextFormField(
            decoration: const InputDecoration(
              labelText: 'TextFormField with maxLines: 1',
              hintText: 'TextFormField with maxLines: 1',
              border: OutlineInputBorder(),
            ),
            maxLines: 1,
          ),
          const SizedBox(height: 16),
          TextFormField(
            decoration: const InputDecoration(
              labelText: 'TextFormField with maxLines: 2',
              hintText: 'TextFormField with maxLines: 2',
              border: OutlineInputBorder(),
            ),
            maxLines: 2,
          ),
        ],
      ),
    );
  }
}

```
</details>

Sounds like no one can reproduce the original issue.  If anyone else sees this problem please comment!

I am continuously testing scribble features but it can't still work on both the latest master and stable channel.
Note that this issue happens with CupertinoTextField and TextField but sadly only in my environment.
@shm40 Thanks for posting!  Can you also post the device you're using and sample code (if there's anything that could be special in your code)?
@fbcouch Any idea about the bugs above?  I think there's nothing to configure related to language/locale in UIScribbleInteractionDelegate etc...  Weird that those parameters affect it.

The locale thing seems odd, I'm not sure why that would affect anything, other than maybe something is different about how the text is coming in from the OS?

For the issue with the Japanese character at the start of the input, I wonder if there's something about the character bounding box that is throwing it off. I wonder if the same thing would happen with an emoji or if it's specific to Japanese characters?

The same thing happened when entered emoji(😌) and when doing scribble in the video, I got the below message in the console.

`[PencilTextInput] ResultCommand 0x280e98080: Could not process result replacing range {1, 8} because it is out of bounds.`

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/125345277/6e6eb935-8d05-4535-b0ab-d37d5b5af4fe

It's suspicious that emojis don't work either.  I wonder if anything in the scribble PRs (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/97437, https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/24224) didn't handle extended grapheme clusters properly.

Same problem here:

Advanced scribble functions, like selecting, deleting and inserting spaces, do not work when `maxLines` is set to `1` or to `null`.

I tested it with Flutter 3.10.6 and 3.13.0-0.2.pre on an iPad Pro (11") with iPadOS 16.5.1. For me, the `supportedLocales` setting doesn`t seem to make any difference.
@fdaugs Is that with plain English text with no emojis or CJK characters?
@justinmc Actually german, but in my test only basic latin characters.

I just re-tested this on 3.13.9 (latest stable at this time) and didn't have any trouble deleting an initial Japanese character or emoji. One interesting thing that I did notice with emoji (but not the Japanese character from above, though maybe it would happen with a more complicated one), is that Scribble seems to treat any text after the emoji as a single unit in some cases – I can insert placeholders (long hold with the pencil), but not insert spaces (draw a vertical line between characters).

issues.title: [Windows] Incorrect character deletion in right-to-left texts
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

Type rtl text to multiline textfield :
(DO NOT COPY AND PASTE)

بابا آب داد
با مهربانی
4طتانا جمعا 4واتا +یکسوییت 20متری
بنبابا 40ساله

### Expected results

The letter to the right of the cursor should be deleted.
(It should be noted that when I copy the text from above and paste it in the text field, everything works correctly.)

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/32570964/b3d09305-3b20-4388-b938-2ce451217a79



### Actual results

But when I type the rtl text , the letter on the left side of the cursor is deleted.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/32570964/31f9ee2d-3aeb-441a-8568-97dcf7fdefe7



### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter_localizations/flutter_localizations.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      locale: const Locale("ar"),
      localizationsDelegates: const [
        GlobalMaterialLocalizations.delegate,
        GlobalWidgetsLocalizations.delegate,
        GlobalCupertinoLocalizations.delegate,
      ],
      supportedLocales: const [Locale("ar")],
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
      ),
      home: const MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Demo Home Page'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyHomePage({Key? key, required this.title}) : super(key: key);

  final String title;

  @override
  State<MyHomePage> createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(
        title: Text(widget.title),
      ),
      body: const Center(
        child: Padding(
          padding: EdgeInsets.all(16),
          child: TextField(
            maxLines: 1000,
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

```

</details>

I tried  `20متری`  in an RTL paragraph but couldn't reproduce the issue. @mohammadjalalis could you provide more detailed instructions to help us reproduce this?

In single-line mode, everything is OK.
But in multi-line mode, when we decide to delete a letter, usually the next letter is deleted. And in general, the placement of the cursor in multi-line mode in right-to-left texts is very problematic.

Video description:

From second 1 to 25, you can see that everything is correct in single-line mode, and when we try to remove the letter "ر", the same "ر" is removed.

From 25 to 1:40, we type a three-line RTL text(Persian language), when we decide to remove the letter "ر" in the second line, the letter "ی" is removed instead, which is incorrect.

From 1:40 to the end with the arrow keys on the keyboard, when we move the pointer over the text, the cursor is not in the right place, and sometimes it moves three times on some letters. For example, in the first line, the cursor moves three times on the word "آب" and the letter "ب".
But the interesting thing is that in the last line, the cursor moves completely correct and is placed at the end of each letter and spaces.


https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/32570964/12e18d3a-a5a0-42b2-8080-e566a11a4e5c

Tip:
I recently noticed an issue, that is, when we type the same text, for example, in the GitHub editor and copy it to the Flutter Textfield, everything is exactly as we expect.
The reason is the addition of "\r" before "\n". 
I hope this tip helps.
In this video, I copied and pasted the following text into the Textfield.

بابا آب داد
20 متری
تا ابد

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/32570964/6e563a8e-4fcc-4f6f-9d1c-1e45ee6cdfce
![image_2024-01-01_13-25-52 copy](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/32570964/2ce71965-ff99-444c-b011-78c7c3abca87)

Thanks for the detailed repro! When the caret offset is 19 the caret should be placed after "ی" 

![image](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/31859944/bb15537b-44d4-4d4a-b402-b3390b285fbb)

So this appears to be a caret placement issue.

Ah I reproduced this on Windows. 
![image](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/31859944/caccd4c7-5aad-4fb1-8973-c2f584292d27)

It's interesting that this is platform specific since the cursor placement logic in the framework is supposed to be platform-agnostic. I doubt this has to do with DirectWrite. Maybe this has something to do with CRLF.

Maybe related: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/140739

When we use the built-in font on Android, everything works fine; however, there are issues when we use a custom font.
In this case, the caret is not positioned correctly and the selection of words is incorrect.

issues.body:
I'm developing an app that doesn't really need the clipboard permission, but I can't get rid of the system alert of clipboard reading. ## Ways I have tried to fix the problem - switch to master channel - run `flutter upgrade` - set `enableInteractiveSelection` flag to `false` in TextField widget Nothing has yet worked on my device nor my friend's ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Connect a XiaoMi device with MIUI 13 installed 2. Execute `flutter debug` on the code sample 3. Copy any text from another place 4. Open the debug app just built and the alert pops up **Expected results:** Textfield without reading clipboard **Actual results:** Textfield still reading clipboard everytime it inits

@bleroux Why exactly is this still a problem if we are using hasPrimaryClip now? Do XiaoMi devices still show the dialog even when hasPrimaryClip is called? If that's true then I'm on board with just always showing Paste on Android.

I would think it should work and not show the dialog now though. On the framework side [hasStrings is used](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/048e38976159022a0baa673bc018a5c1eae93e78/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/text_selection.dart#L3486) to decide to show Paste or not, and in the engine with https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/27215 it looks like that should only call hasPrimaryClip now.

> @bleroux Why exactly is this still a problem if we are using hasPrimaryClip now? Do XiaoMi devices still show the dialog even when hasPrimaryClip is called? If that's true then I'm on board with just always showing Paste on Android.

@justinmc Unfortunately I don't have such a device to look more closely.
I came to this issue because of some recent reports (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/153750 and https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/153749), and I saw that some comments above are somewhat recent (2024).

> I would think it should work and not show the dialog now though. On the framework side [hasStrings is used](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/048e38976159022a0baa673bc018a5c1eae93e78/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/text_selection.dart#L3486) to decide to show Paste or not, and in the engine with [flutter/engine#27215](https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/27215) it looks like that should only call hasPrimaryClip now.

Yes you're right. I looked more closely at the code. At first I thought that `hasStrings` was just returning true when there is something in the clipboard and then that a call to `getClipboardData` was made to see if the content is a text. But I was wrong, `getPrimaryClipDescription` is called to get this information and it is not supposed to trigger any security concerns (based on https://developer.android.com/develop/ui/views/touch-and-input/copy-paste#PastingSystemNotifications).

It looks like, on some devices, there is another layer of security which flags those calls to `hasPrimaryClip` and/or `getPrimaryClipDescription`.
Ideally, it would be great to see what happens if a we create a native Android app that calls `hasPrimaryClip` and `getPrimaryClipDescription` many times. My bet is that the warning would be the same of those devices.

Not sure how to proceed. Would it make sense to always show 'Paste' to accomodate those devices security check? This will apply to all devices, not sure about the impact for end users seeing a paste button when there no valid content to paste.

For reference:
- Engine [implementation for `clipboardHasStrings`](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/main/shell/platform/android/io/flutter/plugin/platform/PlatformPlugin.java#L602-L616)
- Engine [implementation for `getClipboardData`](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/main/shell/platform/android/io/flutter/plugin/platform/PlatformPlugin.java#L529-L593)

Can we remove the loop call 'getPrimaryClipDescription' from didUpdateWidget, as it may cause permission misjudgment and compliance issues on certain platforms.

issues.title: InputDecorator appears not to expand for entire widget
issues.body:
I noticed that when you set `TextField`'s input decoration border to `OutlineInputBorder` it does encompass it's full size.

`TextField ` example: https://dartpad.dev/?id=9fdc5bbe62239c8912ced4d1677abe89

comments: Hey @NebulaFox. I can see the outline around the text field in your sample on Dartpad. But it doesn't seem to have a focus border. Is that the issue you're raising here?
Hey @exaby73 No. The problem is the `TextField` is filling the entire space on the screen, but the `OutlineInputBorder` doesn't. Here is a screenshot to demonstrate the problem.

Looking at the devtools, and your screenshot, the decorator seems to be covering the screen, which makes sense since it's in an `Expanded` widget. If you want the `TextField` to expand too, you can wrap the `TextField` in a `SizedBox.expand`

Thanks for your reply @exaby73.

It is good to see there is a working solution to have the `InputDecorator` be the same size as an expanded `TextField`, but it's a shame that it is not the `TextField`'s internal `InputDecorator`.

Is the solution then to not use the internal `TextField`'s `InputDecorator` but wrap the `TextField` with an `InputDecorator` and then expand the `TextField`?

Example: https://dartpad.dev/?id=7550d138a36a1693031d9c3534a4d053

The `_Decorator` in the screenshot is from the `TextField`'s internal `InputDecorator`.

I think there might be some kind of conflict between the `expands` parameter and the Expanded widget, like what we expect to happen when `expands` is false but it's inside of Expanded, etc.

The fact that you can click below the border to focus the field does seem like a bug...  I'm not sure exactly what the solution should look like in every case, though.

Here is the original PR where I introduced `expanded`, for the record: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/27205.

@danagbemava-nc have you managed to repro this? Can we have a (more) minimal/cleaner repro case than what OP provided, thanks!
Hi @ditman, I haven't been able to reproduce this. It was/is sporadic and doesn't seem to happen in debug mode, only in production

I'm facing the same issue too where my spacebar does not work inside a textfield in release mode, but works in debug mode. My flutter version is 3.22.2, and I am using Google Chrome. I encountered this bug when using shift + tab to traverse the textfields, and suddenly my spacebar does not work in the textfield.

I can't find the comment this was suggested in, but what worked for me (on Chrome) was:

1.  Making a backup copy of the web folder.
2. Deleting the web folder.
3. `flutter create . --platforms web`
4. Putting back the necessary sections of index.html from my backup.
5. Cleaning the project.

I'm not sure if it was just the clean that did it, but the new default index.html was much shorter than the one I had previously, not sure if there was something there causing the problem. Now space bar works in profile and release mode, and without having to modify `WidgetsApp.defaultShortcuts`.

@huycozy and @anthonymoretti you saved my day. Thank you! I had been struggling with this issue for about a month with my production app that occasionally loose the ENTER and SPACE keys and required hard refresh affecting the user experience in a very bad way. The steps outlined by @anthonymoretti solved the issue. I had custom bootstrapping for web using index.html with service workers and onEntrypointLoaded etc. This code was there in index.html from the first version of app in Dec 2022. I am afraid some changes in the flutter web caused this issue (3.7 worked fine. upgrade to 3.32 caused this issue). When recreated index.html for the web, it is plain and simple with the default web bootstrapping. Many thanks @anthonymoretti for the workaround.
Hi @RoarGronmo, are you by any chance using a custom init script in your index.html? Does https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/155073#issuecomment-2888091372 help in your case?

issues.title: TextInputConnection updateConfig does not update action button
issues.body:
Software keyboards have an "action button". It often shows a message like "Done", "Search", or shows a newline icon. This button can be configured as needed by an app. Flutter provides an API to configure this button when the keyboard first opens, as well as an API to change the button after the keyboard is already open.

Flutter's API to change the button after the keyboard has opened doesn't work.

Here's a Flutter snippet that provides an initial keyboard configuration and shows the keyboard:

```dart
_inputConnection = TextInput.attach(
  this, // or whatever you have that implements TextInputClient
  TextInputConfiguration(
    enableDeltaModel: true,
    inputAction: TextInputAction.newline,
    // possibly other configurations
  ),
);

_inputConnection!
  ..show()
  ..setEditingState(/* pass in some initial editing value */);
```

Once the keyboard is visible, an app should be able to change the action button using the following code, but the button doesn't change:

```dart
_inputConnection.updateConfig(
  TextInputConfiguration(
    enableDeltaModel: true,
    inputAction: TextInputAction.done,
    // possibly other configurations
  ),
);
```

Expected result: When the 2nd snippet executes, the keyboard changes its action button from a newline icon to a "Done" button.

Actual result: When the 2nd snippet executes, the action button doesn't change.

I've reproduced this issue on:

 * iOS simulator (iOS 15.0)
 * iPhone 7 (iOS 13.6.1)
 * Android emulator (Android 10 - AKA API 29)
 * Pixel 4a (Android 12)

Flutter version:
```
Flutter 2.8.0 • channel stable • https://github.com/flutter/flutter.git
Framework • revision cf44000065 (7 weeks ago) • 2021-12-08 14:06:50 -0800
Engine • revision 40a99c5951
Tools • Dart 2.15.0

```

issues.title: TextField that unfocuses on onTapOutside crashes app when placed in a list of spaced items
issues.body:
When developing my app, I have several widgets in a column that I want spaced out if they can fit in the available space, but I want the user to be able to scroll if the widgets don't fit. This is addressed in [the Flutter cookbook, on how to create a List with spaced items](https://docs.flutter.dev/cookbook/lists/spaced-items).

After following that recipe, I added a TextField to the list using custom FocusNode behavior. I want the user pressing a completion action on their keyboard to save the user's input and unfocus the TextField (done with onEditingComplete). I also want a tap outside of the TextField to cancel the user's input, and revert the TextField to its default value (done with onTapOutside). However, if there is enough room for the entire column when the virtual keyboard is hidden, but not enough room when the keyboard is active (ie. removing the virtual keyboard gives enough extra room such that scrolling is disabled when it's hidden), this combination is dangerous; when the user is focused on the TextField and scrolls downward with a drag that starts outside of the TextField, a FlutterError is raised and the app crashes.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Follow [the Flutter cookbook to create a List with spaced items](https://docs.flutter.dev/cookbook/lists/spaced-items). This creates a column that allows scrolling if the screen can't fit all elements, but equally spaces out the elements if the screen can fit all elements. This is the widget tree that allows this:

    LayoutBuilder -> SingleChildScrollView -> ConstrainedBox (with constraints set to  `BoxConstraints(minHeight: constraints.maxHeight)`) -> Column

1. Instead of using List.generate to generate the list within the Column, hardcode a list that contains a TextField and a few other arbitrary widgets. This list's elements should take up enough room that scrolling is allowed when a virtual keyboard is active, but _not_ so long that scrolling is allowed when the virtual keyboard is inactive. Example column that works on my target device (Google Pixel 2):

    ```dart    
    Column(
      children: [
        ItemWidget(text: 'Item 0'),
        ItemWidget(text: 'Item 1'),
        ItemWidget(text: 'Item 2'),
        TextField(),
        ItemWidget(text: 'Item 3'),
      ],
    )
    ```

1. Instantiate a FocusNode and add it to the TextField. In the TextField's onTapOutside callback, call focusNode.unfocus(). This provides the user a way to stop editing the text field without saving their changes. Example TextField:

    ```dart
    TextField(
      focusNode: focusNode,
      onTapOutside: (_) {
        focusNode.unfocus();
      },
    ),
    ```

1. Run the app on the target device using a Debug build to see Exceptions. Ensure no scrolling is allowed when the TextField is not selected (ie. when the virtual keyboard isn't active). Click on the TextField to focus on it, bringing up the virtual keyboard and allowing scrolling. Then, scroll down with a drag that starts outside of the TextField; this will trigger onTapOutside, unfocusing the focus node, but will raise a FlutterError for a "Build scheduled during frame." To produce the error, you may need to repeat this step a couple of times since it doesn't happen every time.

### Expected results

When scrolling from outside of the TextField, the TextField should lose focus and the virtual keyboard should disappear without any errors, allowing the user to stop editing the TextField without submitting their changes.

### Actual results

A FlutterError is raised with the following information:

```
FlutterError (Build scheduled during frame.
While the widget tree was being built, laid out, and painted, a new frame was scheduled to rebuild the widget tree.
This might be because setState() was called from a layout or paint callback. If a change is needed to the widget tree, it should be applied as the tree is being built. Scheduling a change for the subsequent frame instead results in an interface that lags behind by one frame. If this was done to make your build dependent on a size measured at layout time, consider using a LayoutBuilder, CustomSingleChildLayout, or CustomMultiChildLayout. If, on the other hand, the one frame delay is the desired effect, for example because this is an animation, consider scheduling the frame in a post-frame callback using SchedulerBinding.addPostFrameCallback or using an AnimationController to trigger the animation.)
```

Once the crash happens, the only logs I get are (with xxxxxxxxxxxxxx replacing my application name):

```
I/xxxxxxxxxxxxxx( 6890): Thread[6,tid=6901,WaitingInMainSignalCatcherLoop,Thread*=0x7a819ef010,peer=0x13300228,"Signal Catcher"]: reacting to signal 3
I/xxxxxxxxxxxxxx( 6890): 
I/xxxxxxxxxxxxxx( 6890): Wrote stack traces to tombstoned
Application finished.

Exited (-1).
```

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

This is a complete example to demonstrate the bug. You may need to add/remove some ItemWidgets to match your target device (I've only seen this bug occur when there are enough elements that scrolling is allowed when the virtual keyboard is active, but there ARE NOT so many elements that scrolling is allowed when the virtual keyboard is hidden.)

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(const SpacedItemsList());

class SpacedItemsList extends StatefulWidget {
  const SpacedItemsList({super.key});

  @override
  State<SpacedItemsList> createState() => _SpacedItemsListState();
}

class _SpacedItemsListState extends State<SpacedItemsList> {
  final FocusNode focusNode = FocusNode();

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      debugShowCheckedModeBanner: false,
      theme: ThemeData(
        colorScheme: ColorScheme.fromSeed(seedColor: Colors.deepPurple),
        cardTheme: CardThemeData(color: Colors.blue.shade50),
      ),
      home: Scaffold(
        body: LayoutBuilder(
          builder: (context, constraints) {
            return SingleChildScrollView(
              child: ConstrainedBox(
                constraints: BoxConstraints(minHeight: constraints.maxHeight),
                child: Column(
                  children: [
                    ItemWidget(text: 'Item 0'),
                    ItemWidget(text: 'Item 1'),
                    ItemWidget(text: 'Item 2'),
                    TextField(
                      focusNode: focusNode,
                      onTapOutside: (_) {
                        focusNode.unfocus();
                      },
                    ),
                    ItemWidget(text: 'Item 3'),
                  ],
                ),
              ),
            );
          },
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

class ItemWidget extends StatelessWidget {
  const ItemWidget({super.key, required this.text});

  final String text;

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Card(child: SizedBox(height: 100, child: Center(child: Text(text))));
  }
}
```

</details>

@Jared-Swislow thanks for the bug report! The `setState` method is supposed to be only called between frames, and that is an important assumption the framework makes. The framework may recover from this but it may also lead to crashes or visual glitches.
The `fyi-framework` label is redundant with the `team-framework` label.

comments:
Hi @PeperMarkreel 
I'm also checking this issue on the latest stable channel but it seems to work as you expected. When navigating to page2b, the TextField will not show the old text from page2a:

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/104349824/6c7977f5-b1c3-40e8-a2fe-e0df3b883728

Can you please share a demo video for this?

Thanks for your reply and checking on the issue. 
I tested and it is reproducible on a Samsung Galaxy S22 and a S9 phone and Pixel_3a_api_33 emulator.

Not reproducible in Edge/Chrome/Windows/MacOS.
I updated the original report with a video.

What is the Android version on your devices?

Thanks for checking. Weird.

I can test more devices in the coming days. For all of the devices except the S9 I added videos to the original report. 

The Galaxy S22 runs android 13,
The Galaxy S9 runs android 10, 
The Android emulator runs android 13.
The iPhone 7 runs iOS 15.7.3
The iPhone simulator runs iOS 16.2

I can also reproduce it in the iOS simulator running iPhone SE 3rd on iOS 16.2 and on an iPhone7 running iOS 15.7.3. 

My main development pc is new and has a brand new install of everything, so no old project/settings/etc. Building from my macbook gives the same problems.

Added video of Nokia G21 phone running android 11 showing the issue. 
Also tested with an ancient Samsung Note II running android 4.4.2 and the bug is reproducible. 

I have built the sample with 2 different windows pc's (of which one is a brand new install of everything) and one macbook.

@PeperMarkreel Thanks for this detailed reports!

Unfortunately, as @huycozy, I'm not able to repro this using the Pixel 3A (API33) emulator
Can you check that the code sample is the exact one you used for the videos? I ask this because we both tried on the Pixel 3A emulator and it is very strange that it does not repro on my side.
Maybe you can also check that the code sample behave the same in a newly created flutter project.

Hi @bleroux thanks for the compliment and the check-in :). Very strange indeed. 

I did already try to put the code example in a new project, on both the macbook and my old development PC. And I made sure to copy exactly the code from the report above in these new project. Still the same issue. 

If it only was one pc I would consider reinstalling everything on my side, but since I have the problem with 3 machines of which one is just 2 weeks old and has everything freshly installed and one is a macbook, I struggle to see how all these different configurations could lead to the same error. 

Any ideas how to proceed? 

One thing I have come up with to find the origin is (if you might want to) to try the output of "flutter build apk --split-per-abi" running on one of your emulators/devices. The apk reproduces the issue here on 4 different emulators (pixel3a api29, pixel3a api33, pixel6 api33, pixelC api33). 
If these apk's reproduce the error on your side it has something to do with my build environment. If these do not reproduce the error it's something in the execution environment, right? Alternatively if you don't want to run my compiled code (which I could understand) maybe can you share your apk? 

[app-arm64-v8a-release.zip](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/files/11500529/app-arm64-v8a-release.zip)
[app-armeabi-v7a-release.zip](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/files/11500535/app-armeabi-v7a-release.zip)
[app-x86_64-release.zip](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/files/11500536/app-x86_64-release.zip)

The issue is problematic for our main development. 
It might be a localization (only reproducible in my country/settings) & optimization (reuse of old widget) issue.

@PeperMarkreel Thanks for providing more info. I installed you generated apk (app-arm64-v8a-release) on my device but still can not reproduce this. You may try my generated apk as well here: [huycozy-app-release.apk.zip](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/files/11504301/huycozy-app-release.apk.zip)

I also try building app from Windows machine but still the same result (can not reproduce this).

On my side, the issue is reproducible when running the apk in app-x86_64-release.zip on the Android emulator.

And interestingly, as I wanted to try on another emulated device (Pixel 6), my previous version of the app was still installed and BEFORE installing @PeperMarkreel  apk, I tried  to see if the issue is reproducible, and it was!

Paying attention to the repro steps I followed, I found that the issue is not reproducible if we tap on the Android keyboard 'Done' button and this is what I did before. @huycozy Can you please try to reproduce without closing the keyboard when going back to the home page?

On this video, first half is when the issue does not repro because I tapped on the 'Done' button, second half is when the issue is reproducible:

[bruno_126853_pixel6.webm](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/840911/9e5cf4cd-2f83-49c3-9052-76c955db6b1d)

@bleroux great find! 
I indeed tested without pressing the done button. If I do press the done button, the bug does not occur (in huycozy's and my build), on pixe3a api33 emulator and galaxy s22 device so can confirm :). 

If I do not press the done button on the keyboard but just swipe back the bug is still there.

Glad we found this, a difference in the execution environment it was ;).

Thank @bleroux for the great insight. Now I can reproduce this issue. 

### Additional info
- The issue happens if TextField still keeps the current focus while navigating between pages (can trigger this either by pressing Done or OS back button, depending on devices).

- If I manually `unfocus` when the page changes (`onPageChanged`), the issue can be solved. I wonder if this behavior could be fixed in PageView's lifecycle or if there is a new option/parameter that can give help in this case.

```dart
onPageChanged: (value) {
    FocusManager.instance.primaryFocus?.unfocus();
}
```

Labeling the issue for more insights. This issue is reproducible on the latest `stable` and `master` channels.

@huycozy 

Hello, I would like to submit an issue to the Flutter community. In CJK keyboards, there is a concept of candidate words, which is used to differentiate which word the user ultimately confirms as their input. Typically, when `isComposingRangeValid` is `true`, it means that the user has not yet confirmed a candidate word, and when it is false, it means that the user has confirmed a candidate word, and only then do we call the `onChanged` method. This is crucial in the underlying implementation of TestField for CJK keyboards.

However, there is a problem in the latest Flutter versions 3.10.1 - 3.10.5, where isComposingRangeValid always returns true, regardless of whether the user has confirmed a candidate word or not, making it impossible to determine if the user has finished inputting.

Additionally, this issue seems to only occur when using Apple and Google Chinese keyboards, as other third-party Chinese keyboards have already handled the candidate word situation.

I hope to receive the community's attention and assistance on this matter. Thank you!

你好，我想向 Flutter 社区提交一个问题。在 CJK 键盘中，有一个候选词的概念，用于区分用户最终确认输入了哪个词语。通常情况下，当 `isComposingRangeValid` 为 `true` 时，代表用户还未确认候选词；而为 false 时，代表用户已确认候选词，此时我们才会调用 `onChanged` 方法。这在 CJK 相关 TestField 的底层封装中非常重要。

然而，最新的 Flutter 3.10.1 - 3.10.5 版本中存在一个问题，无论用户是否确认了候选词，isComposingRangeValid 始终返回 true，导致无法判断用户输入是否完成。

另外，这种问题似乎只出现在使用 Apple 和 Google 等相关中文键盘时，因为其他第三方中文键盘已经处理了候选词的情况。

希望能得到社区的关注和帮助，谢谢！

The documentation states that `onChanged` is invoked when the text is changed by the user and it's been like that for a long time now. To avoid breaking existing code I would recommend introducing a new callback (the signature would probably look like `void Function(TextEditingValue oldValue)` or `void Function(TextEditingValue oldValue, TextEditingValue newValue)` on `EditableText`. As a temporary workaround, if I recall correctly you can achieve the same thing using `TextInputFormatter`s. It really isn't the right API to use but should be an ok workaround.

I have submitted a PR to solve this problem, and I think we should still use the `onChanged` callback, the reason can be found here https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/130081#issuecomment-1626899019

issues.title: [Desktop] Incorrect backspace handling with combined Khmer Unicode characters
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample
2. Using a Khmer Unicode keyboard, type កុំ (K + U + Shift + M) into the text field and pressing Backspace to delete each character

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->

Pressing backspace should only remove one character at a time.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->

The incorrect backspace behaviour on Desktop seems to apply to any combined characters, e.g. កី (K + Shift + I), តើ (T + ;), as it seems to consider them as one character.

</details>
cc @justinmc @dkwingsmt
Seems like a word boundary problem. @LongCatIsLooong

#115900 removes backspace/delete handling from the framework on Apple platforms so this should be working fine on macOS/iOS now. Let's see if Linux/windows need the same treatment. For Android, we may need to implement that in dart, maybe as a separate package.

I don't know if I tested this correctly, but I switched the flutter channel to `master` (ensuring it has the fix commits) to see if the bug is fixed, and it seems like it's fixed for iOS (in the simulator at least) but not on Desktop platforms (problem also reproducible on Windows Desktop); there are still more character deletions than necessary. Here's video demoing the issue with the same code as provided above:

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6465682/205489847-2631b539-25a0-4093-a9f0-95e50916301e.mov

@LongCatIsLooong I've tried to dig a bit into this and here's what I can see. On macOS, with Khmer keyboard and typing "n", "Shift+i", followed by a backspace, this is what we get:

flutter: _formatAndSetValue value: |ន| | characters: ន runes: (6035) codepoints: [6035]
flutter: _formatAndSetValue value: |នី| | characters: នី runes: (6035, 6072) codepoints: [6035, 6072]
flutter: _formatAndSetValue value: || | characters:  runes: () codepoints: []

I'm guessing this should look the same on all platforms and would be very common in all Brahmic / Southeast Asian scripts and some others: a consonant plus a vowel (plus optional parts) end up forming a grapheme.

I've tried typing in a few apps and with Khmer the behaviour is different from e.g. Hangul (tried 2-set Korean keyboard) in that:
(1) Khmer: no composing is used so code points stay the same and after typing more characters, pressing backspace deletes them one by one. Hindi/Devanagari yields the same etc.
(2) Hangul: composing is used and it results in syllables being single code points so one backspace (after composing completes) deletes the whole thing

Currently, in the framework's `CharacterBoundary`, `CharacterRange.at()` is used meaning we will always be deleting at grapheme boundary which breaks (1). 

So here's the question: besides surrogate pairs and emoji graphemes, is it really desired behaviour to delete the entire grapheme? Perhaps the solution would be to revert https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/115900 to make sure the behaviour is consistent on all platforms and instead of using `CharacterRange.at()`, delete at surrogate pair boundaries and maybe also advance on ZWJs to handle emojis with modifiers?

Some interesting reading: https://www.unicode.org/mail-arch/unicode-ml/y2019-m10/0091.html.
I can also see some implementations have specific backspace-only behaviour (e.g. https://github.com/xi-editor/xi-editor/blob/master/rust/core-lib/src/backspace.rs).

Ok, after some further reading of the different issues #115900 was aiming at addressing, maybe my suggestion should be updated as follows:
- if the composing range is empty, handle the shortcut in the framework with backspace deleting code points, not grapheme clusters
- if there's a valid composing range, let the IME handle

> So here's the question: besides surrogate pairs and emoji graphemes, is it really desired behaviour to delete the entire grapheme? Perhaps the solution would be to revert https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/115900 to make sure the behaviour is consistent on all platforms and instead of using CharacterRange.at(), delete at surrogate pair boundaries and maybe also advance on ZWJs to handle emojis with modifiers?

Yeah I think it will definitely be a nice improvement if backspace deletes by element instead of by grapheme. But I don't see why consistency between platforms is important in most applications, wouldn't the user just expect backspace to work exactly the same way as it would in other applications on the same platform? If it's possible to delegate backspace handling to the platform/toolkit IMO that feels like a better option than implementing this in the framework for that reason. Also it may not be as simple as dealing with surrogate pairs and zwjs, the implementation should probably also decompose normalized characters too. On iOS the keyboard manager decomposes precomposed hangul syllables and deletes one jamo at a time.

This issue is assigned but has had no recent status updates. Please consider unassigning this issue if it is not going to be addressed in the near future. This allows people to have a clearer picture of what work is actually planned. Thanks!
This issue was assigned to @LongCatIsLooong but has had no status updates in a long time. To remove any ambiguity about whether the issue is being worked on, the assignee was removed.
cc @cbracken Who dealt with this issue for iOS.
This seems related: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/132917. There's also a different selector `deleteBackwardByDecomposingPreviousCharacter`.
The `triaged-desktop` label is irrelevant if there is no `team-desktop` label or `fyi-desktop` label.

issues.title: Flutter should respect custom system-wide text selection toolbar buttons
issues.body: On some platforms, it's possible to write an app that adds a custom button to the system-wide text selection toolbar. For example, here the Android app AnkiDroid has added the "Anki Card" button to my text selection toolbar, and it appears in any app, such as the Google app here. Flutter should respect these added buttons, so that in any Flutter app, the buttons that the user has added to their device show up in the default text selection toolbar just like in any native app. ### Scope This issue does not include allowing Flutter apps to add system-wide buttons. It's about the reverse problem. ### Platforms | Platform | Behavior | Related Work | | --- | --- | --- | | Android | Apps can add custom system-wide buttons ([relevant article](https://dev.to/bigaru/providing-custom-text-selection-actions-in-android-1akc)). Is there a way for Flutter to read what these buttons are and trigger them? | https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/139361 | | iOS | ? | | | Mac | ? | | | Linux | ? | | | Windows | ? | |
comments:
It would be great to have this feature!

Copying what I wrote at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/107578#issuecomment-1430782248, slightly edited:

As an Android user I find the "Translate" button in particular to be extremely useful, any time I'm reading text in a language other than English. (Even if I know the language moderately well, I'll use it regularly for words and phrases I don't understand.) When it's missing in a given app, if it's any app that's heavy on reading text, I'll notice and it's disappointing.

So as a developer I imagine many of my users value it too.

I believe that "Translate" button is added by the Google Translate app, and so falls in this category. (Other translation apps like DeepL add their own text selection buttons, too.)
It looks like the "Translate" menu button does come from the Google Translate app.  I tried uninstalling the app and the button went away on my phone.

Just for fun, I was able to implement this feature in an empty project. I don't know how this implementation should be in Flutter itself but it doesn't seem too hard. You can see my code here: https://github.com/gualse/flutter_android_process_text/

@gualse Wow that is awesome. It correctly displays all the buttons on my phone and they all work. Are you up for contributing this? We'd need an engine PR that adds `textActionList` and `processTextAction` to the method channels, and then a framework PR to use them for the default context menu buttons. CC @LouiseHsu. I believe you've looked into this kind of thing on iOS. Not sure about there but apparently it's doable on Android.

@justinmc let me look into this a little bit more - From what I've seen, I am not sure there is an ios equivalent of Android's PROCESS_TEXT functionality which would allow you to get access to custom actions added by other apps
No problem!  Thanks for showing that it's possible.

@justinmc I have improved [my repo](https://github.com/gualse/flutter_android_process_text/) a bit. Now it closes the toolbar and supports editable actions that apps like [EncryptTextApp](https://github.com/JanmanX/EncryptTextApp/tree/master) provide.

Also, I would like to point out that:
- By default, the flutter toolbar expends to full width but the native toolbar always holds the buttons of user apps inside three dots.
- In modern versions of Android, the native toolbar is fully rounded and its text isn't bold. Its three dot container is restricted by height and scrollable. The items of three dot container have full-width click zone.
@gualse Thanks!  Can you file separate issues for those two bullet points?  I don't think I've seen them before.

@bleroux Are you interested in implementing this?  It looks to be quite high impact for Android users and should only take a small PR in the engine and framework each...  If it's as easy as the code in @gualse's [repo](https://github.com/gualse/flutter_android_process_text/) makes it look.

FYI, I’m making good progress on this and I will try to file a WIP PR, for the engine side, this week (it might lack some tests and comments but the point will be to get feedback, especially on naming).

@justinmc , I’m not sure how to address one technical point which is that the targeted Android API required a change to the app manifest:

```xml
    <queries>
        <intent>
            <action android:name="android.intent.action.PROCESS_TEXT" />
            <data android:mimeType="text/plain" />
        </intent>
    </queries>
```

Do we want this to be added automatically (if yes how?) or should we let Flutter users decide to add this when they want this feature to be turned on (so we will only provide documentation).

Interesting. I wouldn't expect that this would need anything in the manifest that isn't also needed by native Android apps for the same functionality.

Looking at what appears to be the implementation of this menu in the Android framework:
https://android.googlesource.com/platform/frameworks/base/+/android-13.0.0_r67/core/java/android/widget/Editor.java#7755
https://android.googlesource.com/platform/frameworks/base/+/android-13.0.0_r67/core/java/android/widget/Editor.java#7832
it's invoking exactly the same API, making exactly the same call to `PackageManager.queryIntentActivities`. I believe that code runs in the app's JVM, so I'd expect the system isn't able to give it any special privileges compared to the app's own code when it comes to permissions and the need to declare things in advance in the app manifest. But I'm not an expert in this area and may be missing something.

What happens if you leave that element out of the manifest? Does the query stop finding the services it should find?

@gnprice Many thank for the links to Android implementation!

> What happens if you leave that element out of the manifest? Does the query stop finding the services it should find?

For the moment, I tested this only on one device (Pixel 7), when I remove the `<queries>` element I get no result, when I add it, I get two results on this phone (Google Translate and probably Voice Over).

Reading the Android documentation for `<queries>`, several activities might be automatically visible: https://developer.android.com/training/package-visibility/automatic.
Maybe we can set up things to comply with one of these rules?

@gnprice `<queries>` literally need to find out 3rd-party apps that can handle `android.intent.action.PROCESS_TEXT` because since Android 11 (API 30) apps can't get the list of installed apps. And even a native app needs these lines, despite they don't exist by default in a new project and I have no idea why. The developers of Android may even have forgotten about `PROCESS_TEXT` because even Jetpack Compose still doesn't support this useful feature. Also, I know that `PROCESS_TEXT` was introduced in Android 6.0 (API 23), when there was no installed apps restriction. Maybe of that's why there is no system API that doesn't required add those `<queries>`.

In my opinion, in Flutter those `<queries>` lines should be in the manifest of a new project because it will allow other developers remove these lines if for some reason they don't want them. I don't want these lines to be added manually, because other developers may not know about this. (For example, I don't have any experience with iOS native development and I even never had my own iOS device, so I will be surprised if I have to add some lines in my app for a usual feature like this). I also don't see any reason not to do this, such as the Google Play restrictions. But I could be wrong, this is just my opinion.

### UPD:
Okay, maybe default isn't a super great idea because not all apps have a lot of selectable text. Basically, only social ones have it. But if the app has selectable text it should have those `<queries>` that may developers may forget.

I think it's quite reasonable to add this to the manifest if we find that that's what we need to do in order to make this feature work. Probably that would mean putting it in the template for a new project (`packages/flutter_tools/templates/app_shared/android.tmpl/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml.tmpl`), and perhaps also having an auto-migration to try to add it to existing projects at `flutter build` time.

I'm still a bit puzzled and wonder if there's something we're missing, though.

> I also don't see any reason not to do this, such as the Google Play restrictions.

I don't see a downside to *having* that element in the manifest. But there is a reason it'd be nice to find a way not to *need* it in the manifest: the manifest is generated from a template and then lives in the app's source tree, and I believe we don't have a way to add something to it that will reliably work for all existing apps. So if we need something in the manifest, then the code will need to handle the possibility it's not there, and some existing apps (i.e. where `flutter create` was run before we added this feature) won't have it.

---

> when I remove the `<queries>` element I get no result, when I add it, I get two results on this phone (Google Translate and probably Voice Over).

Interesting, thanks for the experiment.

The thing that leaves me puzzled, then, is that from just looking around at apps on my phone, I'm not finding any that have an element like this in their `<queries>`, even when their text selection menus do have all the options I'd expect. For example the Firefox app — selecting some text, the menu includes options supplied by Google Translate and DeepL and other apps, but the `<queries>` only has `android.intent.action.SEND`. And I'm running Android 13, and the app's targetSdkVersion is 33, so that should be the latest behavior.

In other words this doesn't seem to always be true:

> And even a native app needs these lines

though perhaps in some circumstances it is.

---

> The developers of Android may even have forgotten about `PROCESS_TEXT` because even Jetpack Compose still doesn't support this useful feature.

This is interesting. @gualse do you mean that if you create a new app using Jetpack Compose, and it has some text in it and the user selects the text, they don't get these custom options?

FYI, I filed https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/44579 to gather some feedback on the overall approach.

@justinmc I have two questions in mind:
- do we stick to the Android naming ("process text")? (I have not investigated if similar API are available on the other platforms).
- should we consider that this feature might belong to flutter/packages?

> I don't see a downside to _having_ that element in the manifest. But there is a reason it'd be nice to find a way not to _need_ it in the manifest: the manifest is generated from a template and then lives in the app's source tree, and I believe we don't have a way to add something to it that will reliably work for all existing apps. So if we need something in the manifest, then the code will need to handle the possibility it's not there, and some existing apps (i.e. where `flutter create` was run before we added this feature) won't have it.

@justinmc I agree with this note. After digging into this a little bit, I'm not sure it's possible to add the element programmatically, and I don't see any downsides (but I would make 100% sure at least of this fact before adding it to the manifest template). Adding a note in the documentation about needing the manifest element should be ok for handling older projects and hopefully, we can add some error handling, too.

@bleroux I say let's stick with the Android naming for now, and I think I'll also advocate for keeping this in Flutter proper instead of flutter/packages.  To me it seems like the vast majority of devs will want this out of the box.  Making this a package would probably result in lots of Flutter apps getting shipped without support for some user's favorite context menu button, just due to the develop not knowing they needed the package.

Actually, what is the default behavior on native Android apps?  I just tried sticking a text input in a blank Android app and running it on my phone, and I don't see any of my third party app buttons.  Maybe I was wrong above and if Android is treating this as something that the developer needs to manually activate then we should too?

*Edit*: And indeed, adding the `queries` block [from above](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/107603#issuecomment-1667742390) to my app made it show all of the third party buttons.

If there's no downside to adding the `queries` block, I would probably add it anyway, as that will lead to a better use experience.

IIRC we have logic to automatically add the NETWORK permission during debug mode, we might be able to automatically add this too?

@gualse Fascinating, thanks for the experiment. I reproduce your results. To be explicit:
* Without `<queries>` in the manifest, neither input has custom actions.
* With that `<queries>` element in the manifest, the native `EditText` has custom actions, but the Jetpack Compose `TextField` still lacks them.

Which also agrees with @justinmc's findings at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/107603#issuecomment-1678205375 .

It's interesting that the Jetpack Compose `TextField` lacks this feature. That suggests that it's more of a ground-up rewrite of the Android SDK's component library than I'd realized. For all their end users' sake, I hope they do notice this missing feature and fill it in.

> The developers of Android may even have forgotten about `PROCESS_TEXT` because even Jetpack Compose still doesn't support this useful feature.

I think the Android team is very large, and so are the Android codebase and the overall Android feature set. Quite possibly nobody on the Jetpack Compose team knows about how this feature works, because there's so much else for them to think about. But somewhere off on another team within Android there are still probably others who are well aware of it.

---

There's still a mystery here, because as I mentioned [above](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/107603#issuecomment-1670085219), some of the apps I use do get these custom actions in their text selection menus, and without having that query in a `<queries>` element. 

… Oh. Oh, I see. I think the point is that the `<queries>` element doesn't control what queries you can get results for; it only controls what packages you can see. You'll then see those packages when you make any arbitrary query that should match them.

And those other apps do have `<queries>` in their manifest, just not referring to `PROCESS_TEXT`. For example here's the cleanest one, from [the Firefox app](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=org.mozilla.firefox) mentioned above:
```xml
    <queries>
        <intent>
            <action android:name="android.intent.action.SEND" />
            <data android:mimeType="text/plain" />
        </intent>
    </queries>
```

Lots of apps provide a `SEND` intent-filter; in fact, nearly all of the apps I've looked at that provide `PROCESS_TEXT` also provide `SEND`. That means they'll be visible to the Firefox app, for whatever queries it makes, including `PROCESS_TEXT`.

Confirming that understanding, if I put that version of `<queries>` into the test app, the `EditText` still gets nearly all the same options in its text selection menu, just like Firefox does; in particular it gets all the apps that provide `PROCESS_TEXT` and also provide `SEND`.

Moreover,  to eliminate any possibility that the `EditText` is responsible for doing something fancy: if I do a query for `PROCESS_TEXT` myself, just like the one in https://github.com/flutter/engine/pull/44579 :
```kt
                val intent = Intent().apply { action = Intent.ACTION_PROCESS_TEXT; type = "text/plain" }
                val resolved = packageManager.queryIntentActivities(intent, 0)
                Text(text = resolved.joinToString { it.activityInfo.packageName })
```
then I get the exact same list of packages that's reflected in the `EditText` text selection menu. That's true for each of the three `<queries>` branches of the experiment: no `<queries>`, a `<queries>` with `PROCESS_TEXT`, or a `<queries>` with `SEND`.

For us, the bottom line is that in order for the `PROCESS_TEXT` query to work, we'll indeed need something in `<queries>` in the manifest. And naturally it should be for the same query we actually want to make.

I'm actively working on the Android side of the feature, see https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/139361.

Removing myself from this broader issue because there are no short term plan to implement this on the other platforms.

@bleroux Great to hear! Looking forward to it.

FWIW I'm not sure this is a feature that any platforms other than Android have. Specifically:
* iOS: On an iOS device that has both Anki and Google Translate installed, if I select some text I don't get an item in the toolbar for either of those. (Both of the corresponding Android apps do create such a toolbar item.)
* Linux: There isn't even a system text-selection toolbar in the first place, at least not in my experience as a Linux user. (And I do use Gnome, which is the mainstream window manager.)
  * Just selecting text doesn't cause any kind of menu to appear.
  * Right-clicking often does show a menu, which is the closest analogy to the text selection toolbar of Android or iOS. But between Chrome, Firefox, Android Studio / IntelliJ, and the Gnome Terminal app, no two of those four right-click menus look alike. It seems like it's pretty much independently created by each app respectively.

So I'm pretty sure this feature doesn't exist on iOS or Linux. Not sure about macOS or Windows; I'm away from my Mac so can't immediately check there.

If the underlying feature is indeed Android-only, it'll probably make sense to close this issue once it's done for Android.

@gnprice Interesting feedback, thanks.

Because I'm actively working on the Android implementation (and hope to complete it soon), I wanted to decouple the Android implementation from this issue because this one requires some researches to find out if this is applicable to other platforms.

Your comments related to iOS and Linux are very valuable, this is the kind of analysis we need to understand if this issue is Android only or not.

I'm too a Linux user, working from time to time on Mac or Windows. On macOS, there is a 'Services' sub-menu in the text selection menu, I don't know if applications can contribute to it (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Services_menu).

issues.title: [Windows] Keyboard shortcuts stop working after modifier key repeat
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample
2. Type some text with spaces, press Ctrl+Z to undo (works OK)
3. Press any modifier key (e.g. Ctrl) or a combination of modifier keys (e.g. Shift + Win) and hold for a short while
4. Press arrow keys to move around text, Ctrl+Z again to undo more edits and other shortcuts

**Expected results:** Keyboard shortcuts work
**Actual results:** Shortcuts stop working and the following errors are shown in debug mode:

import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {

  @override

      theme: ThemeData(primarySwatch: Colors.blue),
      home: const MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Demo Home Page'),

}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyHomePage({super.key, required this.title});

}
